gpu: AMD Radeon RX 7900 XTX (gfx1100, LLVM 15.0.7, DRM 3.52, 6.4.9-1-MANJARO)
notes:
To fix, you need to force TF2 to use compatibility and let it update. After it's finished,  turn it off and let it update again. And afterwards, TF2 starts again in native mode.

verifiying game files does nothing and you have to do this everytime you restart your PC. I'm not sure yet what causes this strange behaviour, but I hope it helps for anyone else wanting to play TF2 on Linux.

notes: The listed launch options worked for a few weeks, but they no longer work. Very infuriating how inconsistently this game works since VAC servers started booting Proton clients. Proton used to be the saving grace of this game. It ran so much better and didn't have any of the bugs of the native version. Now neither work and if you get it working for a week, the next week it stops workign again. RIP Linux gaming (2022-2023)

notes: used to work native for me a few months ago. uninstalled, tried to play today  and it doesn't join a match. if using native(no proton) or experimental - it doesn't load into the menu/ or crashes after a few seconds in the main menu, trying to open shift+tab overlay crashes it too.
title: Team Fortress 2

notes: The game installs and launches just fine. It also goes through the training just fine. It never starts a casual game, though. It always crashes the second I click on “join” no matter what launch options I try or whatever proton/GE-proton I choose. Also, I'm never able to join a single community server, it keeps complaining about something “insecure”, hence the “-secure” I have in the launch option. What a shame that a game that is native to Linux never runs on Linux, but runs without a hitch on Windows. :(
